items.why: Another classic faux ami. Spanish 'constipado' refers to nasal congestion (a cold). The medical 'constipated' is 'estreñido'. Confusion can lead to awkward medical conversations.

items.q: Why does 'inglés' have a written accent?
items.correct: It stresses the final syllable while ending in -s, which is unusual.
items.misconception: All Spanish words have accent marks.
items.why: Students treat accents as random. They follow STRESS RULES: words stressed normally don't need accents; the accent shows when stress falls outside the default position (or distinguishes homophones).

items.q: How do you say 'My sister is embarrassed' in Spanish?
items.correct: Mi hermana tiene vergüenza / está avergonzada.
items.why: Notorious mistake when discussing family/relationships. 'Embarazada' = PREGNANT, not embarrassed. To say embarrassed: tener vergüenza or estar avergonzado/a. Saying your sister is pregnant when you mean embarrassed causes laughter.

items.q: How many countries share the Amazon?
items.why: Correct answer: 9. “12” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: Sostenibilidad. “Sociedad” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Paris Agreement target maximum warming degrees C?
items.why: Exact answer: 2. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
